verses.text: A man born blind; who sinned? 'neither,' but that the works of God should be manifest. Not punishment. His blindness was an occasion for God's glory.
A man born blind; who sinned? 'neither,' but that the works of God should be manifest. Not punishment. His blindness was an occasion for God's glory.
A man born blind; who sinned? 'neither,' but that the works of God should be manifest. Not punishment. His blindness was an occasion for God's glory.
I must work while it is day; 'as long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.' Time to work. Christ uses His hour to do good.
I must work while it is day; 'as long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.' Time to work. Christ uses His hour to do good.
He makes clay, anoints the eyes, 'Go, wash in the pool of Siloam'; he washes and sees. Obedient cure. He is healed in doing as Christ said.
He makes clay, anoints the eyes, 'Go, wash in the pool of Siloam'; he washes and sees. Obedient cure. He is healed in doing as Christ said.
The neighbours wonder; 'A man that is called Jesus... opened mine eyes.' Plain testimony. He tells simply what was done for him.
The neighbours wonder; 'A man that is called Jesus... opened mine eyes.' Plain testimony. He tells simply what was done for him.

The neighbours wonder; 'A man that is called Jesus... opened mine eyes.' Plain testimony. He tells simply what was done for him.
The Pharisees are divided, for it was the sabbath; some say He is not of God. Blind to mercy. Their rule blinds them to the miracle.
The Pharisees are divided, for it was the sabbath; some say He is not of God. Blind to mercy. Their rule blinds them to the miracle.
The Pharisees are divided, for it was the sabbath; some say He is not of God. Blind to mercy. Their rule blinds them to the miracle.
The Pharisees are divided, for it was the sabbath; some say He is not of God. Blind to mercy. Their rule blinds them to the miracle.
The healed man says, 'He is a prophet'; the Jews question his parents. Pressed witness. They seek any way to deny the work.
The healed man says, 'He is a prophet'; the Jews question his parents. Pressed witness. They seek any way to deny the work.
The healed man says, 'He is a prophet'; the Jews question his parents. Pressed witness. They seek any way to deny the work.
His parents fear and say, 'he is of age; ask him,' for confessing Christ meant being cast out. Fearful silence. The threat of the synagogue stops their mouths.
His parents fear and say, 'he is of age; ask him,' for confessing Christ meant being cast out. Fearful silence. The threat of the synagogue stops their mouths.

Give God praise; this man is a sinner; 'one thing I know, that, whereas I was blind, now I see.' Bold reply. He holds to what he has experienced.

Give God praise; this man is a sinner; 'one thing I know, that, whereas I was blind, now I see.' Bold reply. He holds to what he has experienced.
They revile him, 'we are Moses' disciples'; we know not whence this man is. Proud ignorance. They boast of Moses yet refuse Christ.
They revile him, 'we are Moses' disciples'; we know not whence this man is. Proud ignorance. They boast of Moses yet refuse Christ.
A marvellous thing, ye know not whence, yet He opened mine eyes; 'if this man were not of God, he could do nothing.' Plain logic. The miracle proves His divine sending.

They cast him out; Jesus finds him, 'Dost thou believe on the Son of God?' 'Lord, I believe'; and he worshipped Him. Found and believing. Cast out by men, he is sought by Christ.

'For judgment I am come... that they which see might be made blind'; your sin remaineth. Judged by light. The self-sufficient stay in their sin.
